name: Holyoke
description_short: Imperial Credit Union is a member-owned NCUA-insured credit union serving members of the African Methodist Episcopal (AME) Church in Illinois and parts of Iowa, offering savings accounts, share-secured loans, and special loan programs.
description_long:
Imperial Credit Union is a volunteer-run, non-profit credit union based in Springfield, Illinois, with the distinction of being the oldest continuously African American-owned business in Springfield. Founded to serve members and families of the African Methodist Episcopal (AME) Church of the Illinois Conference Branch, ICU operates as a mission-driven financial institution dedicated to providing banking services to underserved and unserved communities who may face barriers to traditional financial institutions.

The credit union offers core savings and lending products including NCUA-insured savings accounts (up to $250,000 in coverage), share-secured loans for members with $500+ in deposits, and specialized loan programs such as their 10/10 Loan program ($1,000 at 10% APR with up to 12 months repayment). Members can access their accounts through phone, email, PayPal withdrawal requests, and can purchase gift cards in denominations from $10 to $1,000. The organization is staffed by an interim manager, assistant manager/loan officer, and a board of eight volunteer members.

What distinguishes Imperial Credit Union is its explicit focus on serving a specific faith-based community that has historically faced economic barriers, combined with its volunteer governance structure and emphasis on financial education (offering guidance on savings accounts, emergency funds, and sound financial habits). The organization maintains transparency about its leadership, mission alignment, and accessibility, positioning itself as a trusted alternative to traditional banks for its membership base.

However, ICU's limited service offerings, small organization size, volunteer staffing model, and restriction to a specific membership base (AME Church members) make it unsuitable for general consumers seeking comprehensive banking services. The lack of information about deposit insurance specifics, business hours, physical locations, or digital banking infrastructure suggests limited operational capacity compared to larger credit unions.
services: Loan officer services and member counseling
Financial education content on savings habits
pros: NCUA insurance covers deposits up to $250,000, providing same federal protection as banks
Share-secured loans available at 5% APR with no credit check, protecting credit scores
10/10 Loan program offers $1,000 at 10% APR with flexible 12-month repayment terms
No threat to credit score on share-secured loans even if delinquent (funds withdrawn from account instead)
Multiple account access methods including phone, email, and PayPal withdrawals
Oldest continuously African American-owned business in Springfield, serving community for decades
Mission-aligned lending focused on underserved populations excluded from traditional banking
cons: Membership restricted to African Methodist Episcopal (AME) Church members, limiting accessibility to general public
Small volunteer-run organization with minimal staffing may result in slow service response times
No information provided about digital banking, mobile apps, or online account management capabilities
Limited service menu compared to full-service credit unions—no mention of checking accounts, debit cards, or business services
No published information about business hours, physical branch locations, or service area coverage
best_for: AME Church members seeking low-cost savings accounts with NCUA insurance protection
Church community members needing small emergency loans without credit checks or score impact
Individuals with limited banking history seeking credit-building through share-secured loans
diagnosis: Imperial Credit Union is best for members of the African Methodist Episcopal Church seeking affordable, mission-driven financial services with credit-union protections and low-APR lending. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to the AME Church community, and the volunteer-operated organization offers limited digital services and operational capacity compared to larger financial institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Holyoke Community — Credit Union in Holyoke, CO | CreditDoc
meta_description: Holyoke Community — NCUA-insured credit union in Holyoke, CO. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
